title: lomatium alatum
intro: Lomatium alatum, also known as Lewis's lewisia or Lewis's lomatium, is a perennial plant in the carrot family that is native to the northwestern United States and southwestern Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory infections and fever, due to its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lomatium alatum include its ability to combat colds, flu, and other viral infections, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of Lomatium alatum can be made into tinctures, teas, and salves, and are often used in conjunction with other herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
